skill.name: enact-firecrawl
skill.description: Scrape, crawl, search, map, and extract website data with the Firecrawl API for LLM-ready research workflows.
skill.summary: Use Firecrawl to turn web pages and search results into structured, LLM-ready content.
skill.author: EnactProtocol

security_audit.summary: The static network and environment findings are mostly true positives for a Firecrawl API wrapper, and no confirmed malicious intent or prompt injection was found. The manifest command interpolates user-controlled parameters into a command string, which needs runtime quoting review before publication. Several scanner hits in documentation, OS keyring text, screenshot wording, and weak-crypto keywords are false positives.

high_findings.title: User-Controlled Command Arguments in Manifest
high_findings.description: The manifest command interpolates action, url, formats, limit, only_main_content, prompt, and schema directly into a command string. If the Enact runtime executes this through a shell or without strict argument quoting, crafted input could alter arguments or execute unintended shell syntax.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The interpolation of user-controlled values is directly visible, but exploitability depends on Enact runtime command execution and quoting behavior not shown in these files.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Transfer to Firecrawl
medium_findings.description: The tool sends user-supplied URLs, search queries, extraction prompts, optional schemas, and crawl requests to the Firecrawl API. This is expected for the skill, but users should treat target URLs and extraction instructions as data shared with a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code makes explicit requests.post and requests.get calls to https://api.firecrawl.dev/v1 using user-provided inputs. This is legitimate functionality, but the network data flow is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Secret Environment Variable Used in Authorization Headers
medium_findings.description: The tool reads FIRECRAWL_API_KEY from the environment and sends it as a Bearer token to Firecrawl endpoints. No evidence found that it reads unrelated secrets, but the API key is a sensitive credential handled by the script.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable name is declared as secret in the manifest and read by get_api_key. The same value is placed in Authorization headers for Firecrawl API calls.

medium_findings.title: Open Web Crawling and Search Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill accepts arbitrary URLs and search queries for scraping, crawling, mapping, searching, and extraction. This is normal for a web research tool, but it can retrieve large or sensitive web content if users provide inappropriate targets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The input schema and implementation both show open-world web access. The risk is contextual because the capability is the stated purpose of the skill.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command findings in SKILL.md are fenced CLI examples for users, not code executed by the skill. They should be treated as documentation false positives, separate from the actual manifest command line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside markdown bash fences and usage examples. They are not invoked by firecrawl.py.

low_findings.title: OS Keyring and Screenshot Text Are Not Local Exfiltration Evidence
low_findings.description: The macOS Keychain hit is documentation about where Enact stores a user-provided API key. The screenshot wording describes Firecrawl webpage screenshot support, not local screen capture or upload from the user machine.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive text and format options. No code in the reviewed files accesses the OS keychain directly or captures the local screen.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptography across SKILL.md, but no cryptographic algorithm implementation or hashing call is present in the reviewed file. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found descriptive text and schema fields at the cited locations, not cryptographic operations. The static pattern appears to match common substrings rather than real crypto use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unquoted Manifest Command Interpolation
dangerous_patterns.description: User-controlled manifest variables are expanded directly into a command declaration. Runtime command parsing must guarantee safe argument separation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The risky interpolation pattern is present, but the files do not show how the Enact runtime invokes the command.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential-Bearing HTTP Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The Firecrawl API key is placed in Authorization headers on outbound HTTP requests to api.firecrawl.dev.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Authorization header construction is explicit in the HTTP request blocks. This matches intended Firecrawl authentication.

content.value_statement: Web research often requires clean content from pages, search results, and site maps. This skill uses Firecrawl to scrape, crawl, map, search, and extract structured information for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Scrapes a single URL and returns Firecrawl content in requested formats.
Crawls a website with a configurable page limit.
Maps URLs from a website without scraping each page.
Searches the web through Firecrawl and returns scraped results.
Extracts structured data from a URL using a prompt and optional schema.
limitations: Sends target URLs, queries, prompts, and schemas to the Firecrawl API.
Long crawls and extraction jobs can time out after the configured polling period.
Results depend on Firecrawl access, site permissions, and remote page behavior.

use_cases.title: Collect Source Material
use_cases.description: Scrape a public article or documentation page into clean markdown for summarization or comparison.

use_cases.title: Audit Documentation Coverage
use_cases.description: Crawl a documentation site with a page limit and review discovered content for gaps.

use_cases.title: Extract Page Data
use_cases.description: Pull pricing, headlines, product details, or metadata from a page using a natural language extraction prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Scrape One Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl to scrape this URL as markdown and summarize the main points: https://example.com/article

prompt_templates.title: Map a Site
prompt_templates.scenario: Find important URLs before deeper research
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl map on https://example.com and identify the most relevant documentation, pricing, and support pages.

prompt_templates.title: Crawl Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl crawl on https://docs.example.com with a limit of 10 pages, then summarize the covered topics and missing areas.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Structured Facts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl extract on https://example.com/pricing and return plan names, prices, included features, and any usage limits.

output_examples.input: Scrape a product page and summarize it.
output_examples.output: A concise summary of the product page, including the title, main benefits, feature list, and source URL.

output: A list of discovered documentation URLs.
A short grouping of pages by topic.

output_examples.input: Extract pricing details from a pricing page.
output_examples.output: A structured summary of plan names, visible prices, included features, and notes about missing or ambiguous details.

best_practices: Use the smallest crawl limit that can answer the task.
Avoid sending private URLs, confidential prompts, or sensitive schemas to external services.
Check robots, terms, and site permissions before scraping or crawling.
anti_patterns: Do not crawl large sites without a clear page limit.
Do not use the skill for private intranet pages or sensitive customer data.
Do not assume extracted data is complete without checking the source page.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires FIRECRAWL_API_KEY as a secret environment variable.

faq.question: What services receive my request data?
faq.answer: The skill sends URLs, queries, prompts, schemas, and authentication to the Firecrawl API.

faq.question: Can it crawl an entire website?
faq.answer: It can crawl accessible pages up to the limit you set for the crawl action.

faq.question: Can it return markdown?
faq.answer: Yes. Markdown is the default format, and other Firecrawl formats can be requested.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive content?
faq.answer: No. Treat all submitted URLs and prompts as shared with a third-party scraping service.
